patch.value:
## Context
.github/workflows/codeql.yml lines 39 and 44:
```yaml
uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3
```

All other workflows use SHA-pinned refs (e.g. actions/checkout@de0fac2e... # v6). The CodeQL actions are run on push, PR, and weekly schedule, so this is an active attack surface.

## Acceptance criteria
- codeql.yml pins both github/codeql-action/init and github/codeql-action/analyze to the current immutable SHA for @v3
- ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts PINNED_ACTIONS check extended to cover CodeQL actions (or a comment explains why it is excluded)

## Evidence
.github/workflows/codeql.yml lines 39, 44; ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts PINNED_ACTIONS object (lines 17-25) does not include codeql entries
Pin CodeQL action refs to immutable SHAs in codeql.yml

codeql.yml uses github/codeql-action/init@v3 and github/codeql-action/analyze@v3 (mutable floating tags) rather than SHA-pinned refs. Every other workflow (ci.yml, auto-release.yml, release.yml, nightly.yml) pins all actions to immutable SHAs per supply-chain security policy (pm-hfny was a closed issue that fixed the other workflows). codeql.yml was evidently missed.

- codeql.yml pins both github/codeql-action/init and github/codeql-action/analyze to the current immutable SHA for @v3
- ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts PINNED_ACTIONS check extended to cover CodeQL actions (or a comment explains why it is excluded)
Fixed: codeql.yml init/analyze pinned to immutable SHA (dd903d2... # v3); added a ci-workflow-contract assertion that codeql.yml has no unpinned uses.
